# Immutable images & fleet rollout: Nix DDIs gated by systemd 261 🔴

**Goal.** Compose a reproducible immutable Disk Image with Nix, deploy it on-disk via iPXE, and let systemd 261 own the rest: measured boot, execution restriction locked to the verity /nix/store, and a fraction-gated A/B rollout with automatic rollback.

**Audience:** you can PXE-install and bake images; now make them immutable, measured, and self-updating  
**Effort:** ⏱ half-day+  
**Prerequisites:** [Zero-touch OS provisioning: PXE installs & galleries](path-zero-touch-provisioning.md) · [OS image factories: two ways to bake a bootable image](path-os-image-factories.md)

| # | Lab | You'll learn | Checkpoint (observable) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | [`linuxboot-uefi-kexec`](../linuxboot-uefi-kexec/) | recap: a UKI (ukify + systemd-stub) fuses kernel+initrd+cmdline into one signed EFI blob — the unit that both iPXE chainloads and systemd-stub measures. | the UKI tier boots off an ESP under OVMF to a shell (as verified in that lab). |
| 2 | [`rhel-bootc-minimal`](../rhel-bootc-minimal/) | recap: an image IS the OS — build it once, install it to disk, boot the image's own kernel. The image-mode mindset the Nix DDI takes further with dm-verity + measured boot. | serial login shows an image-mode boot (`ostree=`) of the image's own kernel. |
| 3 | [`nix-systemd-measured-lab`](../nix-systemd-measured-lab/) | Nix owns reproducible image composition (image.repart → dm-verity root + UKI); systemd 261 owns measured boot (ConditionSecurity=measured-os), execution restriction (RestrictFileSystemAccess=/nix/store), and rollout gating (ConditionFraction=/ConditionMachineTag=) — deployed on-disk to A/B slots via systemd-repart, updated by systemd-sysupdate. | the three pillar directives are wired in units/ (host-checkable); then [YOU-RUN-THIS] on a Nix+KVM host: `nix build` yields a byte-reproducible dm-verity DDI, iPXE+systemd-repart write slot A, `journalctl -u measured-os-check` prints `MEASURED-OS: boot measured` with PCR 11 set, an off-`/nix/store` binary is refused exec, the canary fires on only the tagged fraction of a booted fleet, and a bad `ddi-b` boot-counts back to `ddi-a`.<br>🔎 auto-checkable → marker `PILLARS-OK` 🖥️ |
